title: 關於我們在微算機課堂專題做了一台微電腦那檔事
description:
### rota1001、weiso131
如果在課堂上拿到一塊僅有 1.5KB RAM 的微控制器，你會拿它來幹麻呢？在這個專題中，我們從零開始在 PIC18F4520 平台上打造微型電腦，實作分時多工作業系統、FAT32 檔案系統以及支援 USB flash drive 與 HID 鍵盤的 USB Host 驅動程式。在硬體部份，為克服原生記憶體不足以支撐檔案系統快取的瓶頸，我們在麵包板上使用 latch、d-trigger、binary counter 等邏輯元件實作了記憶體連續讀取電路、共享記憶體與 DMA 等機制，算是為身為資工系的自己重新上了一堂深刻的數位系統課。
## 參展者介紹
 ### 陳麒升(rota1001)
是個來自牛肉湯的 CTF 玩家、業餘競程仔、嵌入式系統與韌體開發者，現在是成大資工大三，曾當過 HITCON 2025 講者，湯匙是世界上最棒的餐具。
### 許育瑋(weiso131)
星街武道館演唱會沒抽到票，在武道館外看直播的星詠者，成大資工大三，最近在研究 sched_ext 、跟 pic18f 的編譯器戰鬥
title_en: About the Time We Built a Microcomputer for a Microcomputer Class Project
description_en:
### rota1001, weiso131
If you were handed a microcontroller with only 1.5 KB of RAM in class, what would you build with it? In this project, we built a tiny computer from scratch on the PIC18F4520 platform, implementing a time-sharing operating system, a FAT32 file system, and USB Host drivers for USB flash drives and HID keyboards. On the hardware side, the built-in memory was not enough for file-system caching, so we used latches, D flip-flops, binary counters, and other logic components on a breadboard to implement continuous memory reads, shared memory, DMA-style mechanisms, and more. It became a deep digital systems lesson for us as computer science students.
## Exhibitor Intro
### Chi-Sheng Chen (rota1001)
A CTF player from the land of beef soup, casual competitive programmer, embedded systems and firmware developer, and currently a junior in Computer Science at National Cheng Kung University. Previously spoke at HITCON 2025. Spoons are the greatest utensils in the world.
### Yu-Wei Hsu (weiso131)
A Hoshiyomi who did not win tickets to the Hoshimachi Budokan concert and watched the livestream outside the venue. Currently a junior in Computer Science at National Cheng Kung University, recently researching sched_ext and fighting the PIC18F compiler.

title: 我預判你的預判：IT攻擊鏈早期偵測
description:
### 貝坦betan、欸冷、Ying
在資安攻防中，當 EDR 跳出告警時，往往攻擊者已經長驅直入。我們是否能在 Cyber Kill Chain 的更早期——如偵查階段或武器化階段就察覺異狀？

​本場議程將以藍隊視角出發，探討如何利用常見的 IT 日誌與流量特徵，識別攻擊者早期的試探行為。我將分享如何建立有效的偵測規則，從雜亂的數據中找出攻擊者的「前搖動作」，真正實現「我預判你的預判」，將威脅扼殺在搖籃之中。
## 參展者介紹
### 貝坦betan
嗨我貝坦

地球 online 20年老玩家，目前一轉職業是資安研究員，未來考慮二轉去當召喚師

技能樹都點在資安了，對威脅情資、漏洞研究、以及如何考出一個香濃的起司蛋糕都很有興趣！



「欸不是，這遊戲的 BUG 也太多了吧？」
### 欸冷

來自輔仁大學資訊安全學系的欸冷，是一位熱衷於藍隊防禦技術的資安探索者。深信「最好的防禦就是看穿攻擊者的下一步」，目前專注於研究 IT 環境下的早期攻擊鏈偵測與威脅獵捕
### Ying

我是Ying，輔大大三，對資訊領域和前端開發方面很有興趣。希望透過資訊視覺化讓使用者更容易上手、降低理解門檻，並提升整體呈現的直覺性與可用性。
title_en: Predicting Your Prediction: Early Detection in the IT Attack Chain
description_en:
### betan, Allen, Ying
In cybersecurity defense, when an EDR alert appears, attackers may already have moved deep into the environment. Can we detect abnormalities earlier in the Cyber Kill Chain, such as during reconnaissance or weaponization?

This session takes a blue-team perspective and explores how common IT logs and traffic patterns can reveal early probing behavior. We will share how to build effective detection rules, extract the attacker’s "wind-up motion" from noisy data, and truly predict the prediction before the threat takes hold.
## Exhibitor Intro
### betan
Hi, I am betan. A 20-year veteran of Earth Online, currently class-changed into a security researcher and considering becoming a summoner next. My skill tree is heavily invested in security, threat intelligence, vulnerability research, and baking a rich cheesecake.

"Wait, this game has way too many bugs, right?"
### Allen
Allen is from the Department of Information Security at Fu Jen Catholic University and is passionate about blue-team defense. He believes the best defense is seeing the attacker’s next move, and currently focuses on early attack-chain detection and threat hunting in IT environments.
### Ying
I am Ying, a junior at Fu Jen Catholic University. I am interested in information technology and frontend development, and I hope to make systems easier to use and understand through information visualization while improving presentation, intuition, and usability.

title: 你是說，這裡有一顆能動的 CPU 嗎？！
description:
### sakinu
前有護國神山製程 2 奈米，今有某大學生手刻 CPU。

大家都聽過 CPU，知道手機、電腦，甚至電子手錶裡都有它。有些人或許學過計算機結構、指令集，甚至在課本裡看過各種方塊圖。

但又有多少人曾經真正「看過」一顆 CPU 的內部？看到它的電路、訊號如何流動，甚至親手從指令層級操作它呢？

在這裡，你不只可以看到一顆真正運作的 CPU，還可以直接動手操作它。我們準備了一系列設計好的關卡，讓你透過輸入指令、觀察執行過程，一步一步理解 CPU 是如何思考與運作的。

如果你好奇這顆 CPU 是怎麼被做出來的——從邏輯電路、資料通路，到指令設計與實作過程中遇到的各種坑與挑戰，設計者本人就在旁邊，隨時歡迎來聊聊這顆 CPU 誕生背後的故事。
## 參展者介紹
### sakinu
高睿（sakinu），成大資工大三，業餘競程仔
對電腦科學和音樂有興趣，最近熱愛打羽球，若有在台南的局可以揪><
title_en: Wait, There Is a Working CPU Here?!
description_en:
### sakinu
The semiconductor industry has 2 nm process technology. Now, a university student has hand-built a CPU.

Everyone has heard of CPUs and knows they exist in phones, computers, and even digital watches. Some people may have studied computer architecture, instruction sets, or seen block diagrams in textbooks.

But how many people have actually seen the inside of a CPU, watched circuits and signals flow, or controlled one directly at the instruction level?

Here, you can not only see a real working CPU, but also operate it yourself. We prepared a series of challenges that let you enter instructions, observe the execution process, and step by step understand how a CPU thinks and runs.

If you are curious how this CPU was built, from logic circuits and datapaths to instruction design and all the pitfalls along the way, the designer will be there and ready to talk about the story behind it.
## Exhibitor Intro
### sakinu
Jui Kao (sakinu), a junior in Computer Science at National Cheng Kung University and casual competitive programmer. Interested in computer science and music, recently obsessed with badminton. Invite me if there is a game in Tainan ><

title: 清大 AI 助教【小 TAI】
description:
### UNO、Ritmo
TAI（Teaching Assistant Intelligence）助教是一款專為學習者打造的生成式 AI 服務。邁入第三年的 TAI，已累積約 4,000 名用戶與百萬則互動訊息，服務範圍涵蓋清大、中央、中正、中興及高雄中學。作為媒合教師、學生與教材的橋樑，TAI 深受頂尖學子青睞。

我們利用上下文工程，賦予 AI 真實的人性魅力：無論是活潨學姊「小 TAI」、傲嬌學妹「TAIKO」還是溫柔學長「阿 TAI」，都能針對解題、討論、讀書等學習任務流暢切換，引導學習者精準掌握知識。本次 SITCON 我們將實際展示小 TAI 的行為設計與互動邏輯。如果你正在尋找「超越生產力、直達深度理解」的學習路徑，歡迎來現場與我們交流！
## 參展者介紹
### UNO
清大教育學院博士班，研究如何不讓生成式 AI 使所有人變笨
### Ritmo
24 歲，是個 Lv.0 的偽全端工程師
title_en: NTHU AI Teaching Assistant: Little TAI
description_en:
### UNO, Ritmo
TAI (Teaching Assistant Intelligence) is a generative AI service designed for learners. Now in its third year, TAI has accumulated around 4,000 users and millions of interactions, serving National Tsing Hua University, National Central University, National Chung Cheng University, National Chung Hsing University, and Kaohsiung Senior High School. As a bridge connecting teachers, students, and teaching materials, TAI is widely favored by top students.

Through context engineering, we give AI vivid human-like personalities. Whether it is the lively senior "Little TAI," the tsundere junior "TAIKO," or the gentle senior "Ah TAI," each can smoothly switch between problem solving, discussion, studying, and other learning tasks to guide learners toward precise understanding. At SITCON, we will demonstrate Little TAI’s behavior design and interaction logic. If you are looking for a learning path beyond productivity and toward deeper understanding, come talk with us!
## Exhibitor Intro
### UNO
PhD student at the College of Education, National Tsing Hua University, researching how to prevent generative AI from making everyone less thoughtful.
### Ritmo
24 years old, a Lv.0 pseudo full-stack engineer.
